draft.title: a side project tracker
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a side project tracker

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building a side project tracker prefer persistent navigation over tab-switching, reducing cognitive load when juggling multiple projects.
facade.label: Sidebar nav
facade.acceptImplies: Explore persistent left-rail layouts, collapsible sections, project filtering without page reloads
facade.rejectImplies: Probe tab-based, top-nav, or full-page navigation patterns instead

evidence.hypothesis: Users building a side project tracker prefer persistent navigation over tab-switching, reducing cognitive load when juggling multiple projects.
evidence.implication: Explore persistent left-rail layouts, collapsible sections, project filtering without page reloads

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers intimate, calming atmospheres over sharp, energetic interfaces
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into soft lighting, rounded corners, muted earth tones, gentle shadows, and a sense of coziness in the tracker interface
facade.rejectImplies: The user may prefer cooler tones, higher contrast, cleaner geometry, or more energetic visual treatment

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers gentle, approachable aesthetics over sharp geometric forms
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward friendliness, accessibility, and modern mobile-first design patterns. Likely comfortable with generous padding and breathing room.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer crispness, minimalism, or structured geometry. Could indicate taste for technical/utilitarian interfaces or grid-based layouts.

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers a friendly, motivational tone that feels like chatting with a supportive friend rather than a corporate tool
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual and encouraging
facade.acceptImplies: Future probes should explore playful language, celebratory feedback, warm microcopy, personal touches, and informal phrasing
facade.rejectImplies: Future probes should explore formal language, neutral tone, business-like phrasing, minimal microcopy, and professional distance

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers snappy, immediate feedback over animated transitions when tracking project status
facade.label: Instant updates
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and directness; likely prefers quick state changes, minimal motion, crisp interactions
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer smooth transitions, easing, or animated feedback to acknowledge changes; wants motion to feel intentional

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer circular progress indicators over linear bars for tracking multiple side projects
facade.label: Progress rings
facade.acceptImplies: Circular/radial designs resonate more than horizontal/linear layouts for this use case; may indicate preference for gestalt/whole-at-a-glance feedback
facade.rejectImplies: Linear or list-based progress encodings are preferred; circular designs may feel decorative or unclear for status tracking

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich visual feedback and status indicators for tracking
Whether user wants quick-access toolbar layout vs nested menus vs card-based organization for project management
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional vs witty/casual voice
Whether user wants calm/focused workspace vs energetic/gamified feel with progress celebrations
Whether user prefers flat list view vs hierarchical tree structure vs visual timeline for organizing side projects
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs smooth transitions vs snappy/responsive feel for task updates

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich visual feedback and status indicators for tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-access toolbar layout vs nested menus vs card-based organization for project management
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional vs witty/casual voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm/focused workspace vs energetic/gamified feel with progress celebrations
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers flat list view vs hierarchical tree structure vs visual timeline for organizing side projects
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs smooth transitions vs snappy/responsive feel for task updates
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users managing multiple side projects need visual hierarchy between active and inactive projects; dark card containers against a light background create clear semantic separation without requiring tab-switching or nested navigation.
facade.label: Dark cards on light
facade.acceptImplies: Explore high-contrast card designs, project status communicated through fill color, minimal borders, cards as primary content units in a sidebar or main feed
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward softer contrast (shadows, subtle grays), explore list-based layouts over cards, prioritize negative space over color blocking
